user_bullets: New Management Pack report in Forms & Templates — one click generates a print-ready PDF with Risk Heatmap, Top 10 Risks, KRI Status, Department Risk Distribution, Issues Summary, and Compliance Summary on company letterhead.

All data shapes validated against server.js management-summary endpoint (heatmap, top_risks, kri_summary, issues_summary.by_priority, risk_distribution_by_dept, compliance_summary.overall).

user_bullets: Super Admin created risks now auto-approve immediately (no manual approval step required).
Risk Library search now returns sector-specific risks — 107 risks across 18 sectors including Logistics, Government, Financial Services, Oil & Gas, and more.
Edit Risk button now available for approved open risks for Risk Managers and above.

user_bullets: Full Arabic / English language toggle — switch the entire app to Arabic with RTL layout in one click.
Dashboard, Risk Governance Documents, and Forms & Templates now fully translated into Arabic.
Sidebar language toggle persists across sessions.
bullets: ManagementSummary.jsx: all KPI strip, section headers, table headers, alert messages, and status labels translated via useT().

user_bullets: New Forms & Templates module — generate professional, print-ready documents on company letterhead for board and audit committee filing.

Accepted Risk Report: select a date range, review all CRO-accepted risks in that period, add a covering statement and per-risk commentary, then print a formatted report with your company logo, risk scores, signature block, and confidential footer.
Restricted to CRO, Consultant CRO, Admin, and Super Admin roles.
bullets: Backend: GET /api/forms/accepted-risks — returns accepted risks filtered by cro_actioned_at date range with CRO user name.

Frontend: FormsTemplates.jsx — template card landing view + Accepted Risk Report config panel (date range, risk table, per-risk commentary, covering statement) + print window with letterhead and signature block.
App.jsx + Layout.jsx wired up with forms-templates nav item in Risk group.

user_bullets: New Risk Governance Documents module — upload, version, and manage board approvals, policies, audit reports, and other governance records.
Documents are organised into categories (e.g. Board & Governance, Policies & Frameworks, Audit & Assurance) with unique Doc IDs in the format CODE-YEAR-SEQ.
Full version history per document — upload new versions without losing prior copies.
Restricted to CRO, Consultant CRO, Risk Manager, Admin, and Super Admin roles.
bullets: schema_v69_risk_governance_docs.sql: risk_gov_categories and risk_gov_documents tables with 8 default categories seeded per company.

Frontend: RiskGovDocs.jsx — category management panel, filterable document table, upload modal, version history panel.
App.jsx + Layout.jsx wired up with risk-gov-docs nav item in Risk group.

user_bullets: Super Admin role added — provides unrestricted access to all modules and features for testing purposes.
bullets: New Super Admin role added to user_companies role constraint (schema_v68).
Backend requireRole() bypass extended to Super Admin.
Frontend: Super Admin added to all role arrays and content conditions in Layout.jsx and App.jsx.
Super Admin available in the Users & Access role dropdown.
Super Admin lands on Risk Register by default and can navigate to all pages without restriction.

user_bullets: Several stability improvements: resolved errors when saving risks and KRIs with certain optional fields left blank.
Issue action items module enabled.
Navigation sidebar refined.
bullets: server.js attachControlsAndMitigations(): Promise.all → Promise.allSettled — enrichment query failures no longer abort the response.
schema_v67_issue_actions.sql: corrected FK column types TEXT → INTEGER to match users.id SERIAL.

user_bullets: Risk Appetite: qualitative-only statements now supported (max residual score is optional).

Risk Appetite: form upgraded with notes field, approved-by role dropdown + approver name, approval date (past-only validation), tolerance band inputs, range slider for max score, live character counter, and pre-fill templates for breach action.
Risk Appetite: breach urgency auto-defaults from appetite level (Zero Tolerance/Low → Critical; Moderate/High → High) and is now required.
Risk Appetite: version history moved from modal to inline collapsible panel on each statement card (Admin/CRO only).
Incident Log: three-option register decision workflow — Link to Existing Risk, Create Risk in Register, or Dismiss with a reason note.
Incident Log: table now shows Register Decision column with linked risk UID badge.
Incident Log: 'Create Risk' navigates directly to Risk Register with the new risk form open; risk is auto-linked to the originating incident on save.

user_bullets: Risk Appetite: breach notification severity — choose Critical (immediate action) or High (action within 24 hours) on any appetite statement.
When a risk newly breaches the appetite threshold, an email is automatically sent to the CRO, risk owner, and relevant Risk Manager with the severity urgency and breach details.
Breach notification level shown as a colour-coded badge on the appetite statement card.

RiskAppetite.jsx: statement card — severity badge rendered with red (Critical) or amber (High) background when set.

user_bullets: New Horizon Scanning module — log and track emerging risks across 6 categories (Regulatory, Geopolitical, Technology, Economic, Environmental, Social).
Radar matrix view plots active signals by time horizon × potential impact so high-priority items stand out at a glance.
AI scan button (CRO/Risk Manager): if an AI API key is configured, the AI scans external sources and drafts candidate signals for review. Drafts must be published before they enter the active list.
Signal detail panel, category badges, escalation highlighting, and convert-to-risk workflow.
Admin → AI Integration: configure an AI API key and provider for your organisation.
KRI Library: risk appetite statement can now be linked to a KRI — tolerance band shown in the form with the board ceiling for context.
KRI breaches now include the linked appetite category in auto-generated issue descriptions.
Risk Register: linked KRIs card now shows the appetite category chip alongside the KRI band.
Escalation engine: new kri_measurement_due trigger alerts when a KRI measurement is overdue.

Risk form auto-saves as Draft in the background after 15 seconds of inactivity — 'Draft saved' indicator shown.

user_bullets: Fixed: Org Roles RACI page no longer throws an Internal Server Error.
Fixed: My Tasks no longer throws an Internal Server Error for any role.
Fixed: Newly-created departments now appear immediately when opening the new risk form.
Fixed: Business Unit selection no longer disappears after picking it in the new risk form.
Improved: Treatment Strategy is now shown on Step 2 (right after Inherent Risk Scoring) and the ISO label has been removed.
Simplified: Tolerance threshold, risk appetite, and risk velocity fields removed from the risk form.
Renamed: 'Owner Department' is now 'Control Owner' (free-text name/email) on the control row.
Added: Control Effectiveness dropdown (Not Tested / Effective / Partially Effective / Ineffective) per control row.
Added: A Mitigation Action Plan is now required when any control is Partially Effective or Ineffective.
bullets: schema_v57_raci_matrix.sql: adds raci_matrix table to the standard migration chain (previously only applied via standalone migrate-raci-matrix.js).
schema_v58_raised_by_dept.sql: adds raised_by_dept TEXT column to issues table (previously only applied via standalone migrate-issues-raised-by-dept.js).
migrate-all.js: SCHEMA_FILES updated to include v56, v57, v58.
CascadingDeptSelector.jsx: useEffect no longer clears selectedBuId when dept is cleared — prevents BU from deselecting itself.
RiskRegister.jsx: Treatment Strategy + Accept rationale moved to Step 2 row; old Step 5 (tolerance, appetite, velocity, accept rationale) removed; steps renumbered 3–8.
RiskRegister.jsx: TREATMENT_STRATEGIES — removed '(ISO Standard Target)' suffix.
RiskRegister.jsx: control row owner_department dropdown replaced with owner text input; effectiveness select added after type.
RiskRegister.jsx: handleSubmit validates that a mitigation action exists when any control is Partially Effective or Ineffective.
RiskRegister.jsx: dept refresh on showForm open (useEffect watches showForm).
server.js: inline control INSERT now stores owner + last_test_result (effectiveness) instead of owner_department.
package.json bumped 2.26.9 → 2.27.0.

user_bullets: Fixed: importing a risk register no longer fails with a database constraint error on every row. Controls are now saved successfully with their effectiveness values.
bullets: controls_lib.last_test_result DB check constraint only allowed 'Not Tested' (schema_v3), but the entire application (import wizard default, test endpoint, ControlLibrary UI) uses 'Not yet tested'. All rows inserted by the import wizard failed with controls_lib_last_test_result_check violation.

schema_v56_fix_controls_lib_constraint.sql: DROP + re-ADD constraint to include both 'Not Tested' and 'Not yet tested', keeping all existing allowed values (Effective, Partially Effective, Ineffective).
package.json bumped 2.26.8 → 2.26.9.

user_bullets: Smart column mapping: the mapper now recognises columns like 'Inherent Risk Likelihood', 'Residual Risk Impact', 'Monitoring frequency', 'Control Description', 'Mitigation Decision', 'Action Plan...', and 'If an Action Plan, Define Responsibility' automatically — even when the column name contains extra words or punctuation.
Business Unit is now a mappable field. Files with both a 'Business Unit' and a 'Department' column will map each to the correct target independently.
Column matching no longer breaks on commas, newlines, or em-dashes in column headers.
bullets: normalise(): upgraded from stripping specific chars to replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g,'') — strips commas, newlines, em-dashes, and any other punctuation that appeared in Qatar Post column headers.

matchScore(): new 5-level scoring function — exact key/label (5), exact alias (4), long-alias prefix match (3), all key tokens present OR very-long alias contained (2), medium alias contained (1). Replaced simple 4-level function.

buildInitialMap(): switched from header-first to field-first iteration with matchScore scoring — ensures 'Department' (exact match, score 5) wins over 'Business Unit' (alias match, score 4) for the department field.
Added business_unit to FIELD_DEFS (risk_register + risks) — falls back to business_unit value if department is empty in importRisksRow backend.

Added missing aliases: monitoringfrequency on control_1_frequency; controldescription on control_1_name; mitigationdecision on treatment_strategy; mitigationdetails on treatment_plan_rationale; riskcateogorylevel2 (typo in Qatar Post file) on sub_category; defineresponsibility on action_1_owner.
package.json bumped 2.26.7 → 2.26.8.

user_bullets: Fixed: second (and third, etc.) controls from continuation rows now appear correctly in the column mapper after upload — the previous CSV parser broke on cell values containing line breaks, which is common in risk register control descriptions.
bullets: parseCSVClient: complete rewrite as a proper RFC 4180 character-by-character parser. The old implementation split on \n before parsing quotes, so multi-line quoted fields (control descriptions with embedded newlines) were torn into fake rows — corrupting all data after the first long description.
The new parser accumulates characters inside quotes including \n, only splitting on \n when outside a quoted field.
End-to-end verified against Draft Risk Register - Finance (1).xlsx: 5 risks parsed correctly, Control 2 (continuation row) fully merged with type/description/frequency/effectiveness fields.
package.json bumped 2.26.6 → 2.26.7.

user_bullets: Risk import mapper: 'Risk Cause' and 'Risk Consequence / Impact' removed as separate fields — replaced by a single 'Risk Statement' field that maps to the risk description, matching how risk registers are actually written.
bullets: FIELD_DEFS risk_register + risks: removed risk_cause and risk_consequence entries; renamed risk_detail label to 'Risk Statement' with expanded aliases (riskstatement, statement, risknarrative, etc.).
Backend unchanged — risk_cause and risk_consequence DB columns remain and will be null for imported records (they are optional fields).
package.json bumped 2.26.5 → 2.26.6.

user_bullets: Risk Register import: if multiple controls (or actions) are listed in a single cell separated by semicolons or line breaks, they are now split into separate control/action records automatically.
bullets: importRiskRegisterRow (server.js): detect newline or semicolon delimiters in control_1_name and expand into control_2_name … control_5_name slots before the insert loop. Same logic applied to action_1_description → action_2/3.
Attributes (type, owner, frequency, effectiveness) for split controls inherit system defaults (Preventive, Manual, Quarterly, Not yet tested) — user can edit after import.
package.json bumped 2.26.4 → 2.26.5.

user_bullets: Risk Register import: continuation rows (extra controls in separate spreadsheet rows) now auto-map correctly in the column mapper — columns like "Control Type_2" and "Monitoring Frequency_2" are automatically recognised.
bullets: FIELD_DEFS.risk_register: added numbered-suffix aliases for control_2 through control_5 fields (name, type, automation, owner, frequency, effectiveness) so merged continuation-row columns (e.g. 'Control Type_2' → normalised 'controltype2') are correctly auto-suggested in StepMap.
Added missing control_3_automation, control_4_automation, control_5_automation field definitions.
Fixed alias ordering — 'controlautomation2' (normalised from 'Control Automation_2') now correctly matches control_2_automation instead of the transposed form.
package.json bumped 2.26.3 → 2.26.4.

user_bullets: New: Smart Import Wizard — upload your own file in any format (CSV or Excel), map your columns to our fields in a visual UI, preview every row before anything is saved, then confirm to import.
Auto-matching: the wizard guesses which of your columns maps to our fields using fuzzy matching — you only need to correct mismatches.
Dry-run preview: all rows are validated against the system before import; errors are shown per-row and will be skipped on final import so valid rows always get through.
Excel support: .xlsx and .xls files are automatically converted to CSV so you don't need to export to CSV first.
ISO 31000 compliant label added to the Download Risks Template button.
bullets: DataTools.jsx: full rewrite — 4-step wizard (Upload → Map Columns → Preview → Done) replacing the old single-step import.
FIELD_DEFS: per-module field definitions with required flag and aliases arrays for fuzzy matching (risks, controls, policies, obligations).
normalise() + autoSuggest(): normalise headers to lowercase stripped strings and match against aliases.
buildInitialMap(): auto-suggests a columnMap from parsed headers on entering step 2.
remapToCSV(): converts parsed rows + columnMap into a remapped CSV using our field names for the backend.
parseCSVClient(): client-side CSV parser supporting quoted fields and commas inside values.
xlsxToCsv(): converts uploaded .xlsx/.xls to CSV using read-excel-file (already in frontend deps).
StepUpload / StepMap / StepPreview / StepDone: step components with StepBar progress indicator.
server.js POST /api/import/:module: dry_run support with BEGIN/COMMIT and per-row SAVEPOINTs — ROLLBACK on dry_run=true, audit only logged on real import.
package.json bumped 2.25.3 → 2.26.0.

user_bullets: Fixed: downloading CSV templates (Risks, Controls, Policies, Obligations) and all exports now work correctly.
Fixed: the 'e is not a function' error on the Import / Export page is resolved.
bullets: api.js getBlob: added credentials: 'include' so the session cookie is sent with template/export fetch requests — without it the server returned 401 and the download failed.
api.js getBlob: changed getToken() to getToken?.() to match the rest of the api client and avoid TypeError if getToken is undefined.
package.json bumped 2.25.2 → 2.25.3.

user_bullets: New: Admin and CRO can now add escalation rules via the + Add Rule button on the Escalation Rules page.
CRO and Consultant CRO now have access to the Escalation Rules page (previously Admin-only).
The Add Rule button only appears when there are trigger types not yet configured; once all 5 are covered it disappears.

EscalationRules.jsx: AddRuleModal component added; + Add Rule button shown when availableTriggers.length > 0; empty-state message added.

Edit access now clearly shows scope: 'Dept only' for Risk Manager and 'Own only' for Risk Champion.
Info tooltips explain each action where the rules are nuanced (e.g., the 3-stage risk approval workflow).
bullets: AccessMatrix.jsx: rebuilt with action-level granularity across 10 module sections; each row is a specific action (not just a page), with Full / Dept / Own / No-access cell types.
Colour-coded badges: green = Full, amber = Dept/Own scoped, grey = no access.
Hover tooltips (InfoTip component) on rows with complex rules.
Your-role column highlighting preserved.

user_bullets: New: Access Matrix page shows which modules every role can access — visible to Admin and CRO roles.
The matrix is grouped by module category and highlights your own role column.
bullets: AccessMatrix.jsx: new read-only page with a colour-coded table of all 7 roles × all modules, grouped by section.
Layout.jsx: 'Access Matrix' nav item added to Admin group, visible to Admin, CRO, and Consultant CRO.
App.jsx: route added for 'access-matrix' page (Admin and CRO roles).

user_bullets: Fixed: the temporary password is now always shown on screen when a new user is added — regardless of whether email is configured.
Fixed: ghost users left over from previously failed attempts are now recovered — their password is reset and the temp password is shown correctly.
Improved: when a user is added to a company and already has an active account elsewhere, the message now correctly says 'log in with existing credentials' instead of 'email sent'.
bullets: server.js: removed `!emailSent &&` gate from the tempPassword response field — temp password is now always returned to the admin for new users.
server.js: added ghost user detection — if a user exists in the users table but has no user_companies rows, their password is reset and a fresh temp password is generated.
UserManagement.jsx: TempPasswordModal now accepts emailSent prop and shows contextual message (email also sent vs. email not configured).
UserManagement.jsx: fallback message when no tempPassword now correctly says 'use existing credentials' instead of 'welcome email sent'.
package.json bumped 2.24.6 → 2.24.7.

user_bullets: Fixed: adding a user as Risk Owner or Risk Manager no longer causes an internal server error on retry.
Fixed: when re-adding a user after a failed first attempt, the temporary password is now correctly generated and displayed.
bullets: server.js: wrapped users INSERT + password_history INSERT + user_companies INSERT in a single DB transaction. If user_companies fails (e.g. role constraint violation), the users row is rolled back atomically — eliminating the ghost user that caused retries to skip temp password generation.
server.js: moved explicit temporary_password policy validation before the transaction open to allow clean early return without an open transaction.
package.json bumped 2.24.5 → 2.24.6.

schema: schema_v54_rename_roles.sql — renames 'Manager' → 'Risk Manager' and 'Approver' → 'Risk Owner' in user_companies and updates the role CHECK constraint
user_bullets: The 'Manager' role has been renamed to 'Risk Manager' throughout the application.
The 'Approver' role has been renamed to 'Risk Owner' throughout the application.
All existing users with these roles are automatically updated — no action needed.
bullets: schema_v54_rename_roles.sql: drops old role CHECK constraint, renames existing DB rows, adds updated constraint with Risk Manager and Risk Owner.
migrate-all.js: schema_v54 registered.
server.js: replace_all 'Manager' → 'Risk Manager', 'Approver' → 'Risk Owner' across all requireRole guards, SQL WHERE clauses, role arrays, error messages, and comments.

Layout.jsx, App.jsx, UserManagement.jsx, RiskRegister.jsx, OrgRoles.jsx, DepartmentField.jsx, TopBar.jsx, EvidenceAttachments.jsx, ComplianceObligations.jsx, ControlLibrary.jsx, KriLibrary.jsx, PolicyRepository.jsx, TrainingLibrary.jsx: role strings updated.
test-suite.js, test-bcm.js, test-suite-extended.js, test-routing-audit.js: role strings updated.
package.json bumped 2.24.4 → 2.24.5.

schema: schema_v53_company_address.sql — adds address column to companies table
user_bullets: Company code is now auto-generated from the company name as you type — no need to invent one manually.
Added an Address field to the company creation and company profile forms.
The warning when switching to Business Units mode is now in plain language.
Subsidiaries now have an Edit button — click it to update name, industry, country, address, and access settings without deleting and recreating.
'Group Access Ceiling' has been renamed 'Parent Company Access'; dropdown options are now Consolidated View, Entity Level View, and Full Access.
Duplicate company names in the same country are now blocked with a clear error message.
bullets: Companies.jsx: suggestCode() auto-fills Company Code from name; address field added to all three forms (standalone, subsidiary, profile).
Companies.jsx: BUG-01 — BU mode warning rewritten in plain language.
Companies.jsx: BUG-03 — 'Group Access Ceiling' → 'Parent Company Access'; dropdown labels updated.
Companies.jsx: BUG-04 — editingSub state + saveEditSub + Edit button + modal for subsidiaries.
server.js: PUT /api/companies/:id extended to accept full profile fields (industry, company_type, country, regulatory_body, fiscal_year_end, description, address).
server.js: address field added to POST /api/companies/standalone, POST /api/companies, GET + PUT /api/companies/current/profile.
server.js: BUG-06 — duplicate name+country check added to both POST company routes; returns 409 with clear message.
schema_v53_company_address.sql: ALTER TABLE companies ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS address TEXT.
migrate-all.js: schema_v53 registered.
package.json bumped 2.24.3 → 2.24.4.

user_bullets: Enabling Business Units mode now takes effect immediately — the Business Units page shows the Add BU form straight away without requiring a logout or page reload.
bullets: Companies.jsx: added refreshMe() call after saveProfile succeeds so the session (and thus activeCompany.has_business_units) is updated in real time.
package.json bumped 2.24.2 → 2.24.3.

user_bullets: When a new user is created and email is not configured, the temporary password is now displayed on-screen in a modal so the admin can copy and share it directly.
bullets: server.js: user-create endpoint now includes tempPassword in the response when emailSent is false.
UserManagement.jsx: added TempPasswordModal component; AddUserForm shows it after creation when tempPassword is returned, blocking navigation until the admin acknowledges.

user_bullets: Single-session enforcement now works in real time: if you log in on a second device, the first device is signed out within 30 seconds instead of waiting for the next page action.

Forgot password no longer requires email to be configured. Enter your email and a temporary password is generated and shown on screen — log in with it and you'll be prompted to set a new one immediately.
bullets: AuthContext.jsx: added 30-second heartbeat (setInterval on /api/auth/me) so Device A detects its invalidated session promptly after Device B logs in.

server.js forgot-password: replaced email-based token flow with inline temp-password generation. Sets must_change_password = true on the account and returns the plaintext temp password in the response.
ForgotPassword.jsx: replaced 'Check your email' UI with a temp-password display card including a Copy button. Shows 'No account found' for unknown emails.
package.json bumped 2.24.0 → 2.24.1.

user_bullets: Full Arabic / English bilingual UI: every page title, subtitle, button, column header, form label, status badge, empty state, and placeholder now switches to Arabic when the EN/عر toggle is activated.

Translations cover all modules: Risk Register, Control Library, KRI Library & Register, Issues & Actions, Compliance Obligations, Compliance Calendar, Policy Repository, Audit Log, Glossary, Import/Export, Training Videos, and all Admin pages.
Arabic text is rendered right-to-left in the main content area; the sidebar always stays left-to-right for navigation consistency.
bullets: translations.js: expanded to 200+ keys covering all pages (audit log tabs, filter labels, column headers, Glossary form, DataTools sections, and per-page titles/subtitles).
AuditLog.jsx: column headers, tab labels, filter placeholder, clear-filters button, loading and empty states all use t().
Glossary.jsx: title, subtitle, search placeholder, add-term form, column headers, custom badge, and no-match state translated.
DataTools.jsx: title, subtitle, Bulk Import section, Export Data section, Standard Controls card, module label, and button labels translated.
PolicyRepository.jsx, ComplianceCalendar.jsx, TrainingLibrary.jsx, RiskRegister.jsx: page titles and subtitles now use t().
package.json bumped from 2.23.1 → 2.24.0.

user_bullets: Security: logging in from a new device now automatically ends any existing session on another device. Only one active session per user is permitted at a time.
bullets: auth.js createSession(): added DELETE FROM sessions WHERE user_id before INSERT to enforce single-session-per-user. Prevents a compromised credential from maintaining a silent parallel session alongside the legitimate user.

user_bullets: New: Arabic/English language toggle added to the navigation sidebar footer (alongside Help and About). Click 'عر' to switch to Arabic; click 'EN' to switch back. Preference is remembered across sessions.

Arabic mode: all navigation labels, section headings, and the Log Out button display in Arabic. Main content area switches to right-to-left (RTL) layout. The sidebar and logout button remain centered and visually stable in both languages.
bullets: Created contexts/LanguageContext.jsx: LanguageProvider + useLanguage hook. Language preference persisted in localStorage under 'grc_lang'.

Layout.jsx: added AR_LABELS and AR_GROUPS translation maps for all 26 nav items and 5 section headings. Language toggle button (EN/عر) added to Help/About row in sidebar footer. main-content div gets dir=rtl when Arabic is active. Logout button label switches to 'تسجيل الخروج' in Arabic.
main.jsx: LanguageProvider wraps AuthProvider at the root so all components can read lang via useLanguage().

user_bullets: Fixed: Scoring Methodology page crashed to a blank white screen after loading. Root cause was a removed API endpoint (/api/categories) being replaced by the SPA catch-all, returning null to the component and crashing the render.
Admin sidebar reordered to match the natural company setup sequence (Branding first, Storage & Health last). Utility items moved to the bottom.
bullets: ScoringMethodology.jsx: replaced removed /api/categories call with /api/risk-taxonomy. Updated category rendering to use new {id, name} object format. Removed add/delete UI (now Admin-only in Risk Configuration).
Layout.jsx: Admin nav group reordered by setup sequence. Ungrouped utility items moved after Admin group.

user_bullets: Admin sidebar reordered to match the natural company setup sequence: Branding → Company Structure → Business Units → Departments → Users & Access → Risk Configuration → Escalation Rules → Email Settings → Storage & Health.
Utility items (Glossary, Training Videos, Audit Log, Import / Export) moved to the bottom of the sidebar for Admin users so primary setup tasks are always visible first.
bullets: Layout.jsx: Admin nav group reordered to reflect setup sequence (Branding first, Storage & Health last).
Layout.jsx: Ungrouped utility items (glossary, training, audit, data-tools) moved after the Admin group so they appear at the bottom for Admin role.

user_bullets: Security: Adding an existing user to a company no longer resets their password — their existing credentials are preserved and they can log in without any action required.
Security: Evidence filenames are now validated against a stricter character set that explicitly blocks control characters (CR, LF, NUL) in addition to path separators.
Security: Risk sub-category deletion now enforces company ownership at the database level on the final DELETE statement (defense-in-depth).
bullets: NEW-01 (MEDIUM, Backend): POST /api/users — when a globally-existing user is re-added to a company, password is no longer reset. Only the user_companies link is created. Prevents any Admin from disrupting another user's access by adding their email to a different company.
NEW-02 (LOW, Backend): Evidence filename regex in validate.js updated from /^[^/\\:*?"<>|]+$/ to /^[^/\\\r\n\0:*?"<>|]+$/ — explicitly excludes CR, LF, and NUL characters.
NEW-03 (INFO, Backend): DELETE /api/risk-sub-categories/:id — final DELETE query now scoped to company_id via subquery for defense-in-depth (ownership was already verified by preceding SELECT).

user_bullets: Fix: Seed Controls Wizard — standard controls belonging to a 'skipped' support function are now correctly excluded (they were silently seeded under the wrong department name).
Fix: Running the wizard a second time on the same company now returns a clear error instead of creating duplicate controls.
Fix: After applying on a company with no existing register, the wizard now advances to the Done screen correctly (previously both the Confirm and Done panels could appear at the same time).
bullets: SC-01 (MEDIUM, Backend): Empty dept-map entry no longer falls back to the seed function name — controls for skipped functions are now skipped.
SC-02 (LOW, Backend): Added idempotency guard on /api/seed-controls/apply — returns HTTP 409 if standard controls have already been seeded for this company.
SC-03 (LOW, Backend): /api/seed-controls/preview now enforces the same 1000-row limit as the main controls import route.
SC-05 (INFO, Backend): logAudit call moved to after COMMIT so audit entries only appear for committed data.
SC-06 (MEDIUM, Frontend): setStep(4) always advances to Done after Apply — removed the stale dual-render path that caused StepConfirm and StepDone to render simultaneously on the fresh-company flow.
files: server.js (seed-controls routes: SC-01, SC-02, SC-03, SC-05)

user_bullets: New: Standard Controls Seeding Wizard — seed 55 Certitude-approved controls across Finance, HR, IT, Legal & Compliance, Procurement, Risk Management, and Facilities in a few clicks during company onboarding.
The wizard handles companies with no existing register (seed directly) and companies with a pre-existing control register (upload, review duplicates side-by-side, choose which to keep, then seed).
Duplicate detection uses name-similarity matching — flagged pairs are shown side-by-side so the Admin can decide to keep the standard control or their existing one.
All four optional fields (automation, testing frequency, evidence required, framework reference) are left blank for the company to complete — nothing is assumed.
Accessible from Import / Export → Standard Controls button. Admin role only.
bullets: seed-controls-data.js: New data module containing all 55 approved standard controls across 7 support functions.

server.js: GET /api/seed-controls (returns full list), POST /api/seed-controls/preview (parse CSV + Jaccard similarity analysis), POST /api/seed-controls/apply (seed selected + import client controls with mapping).
frontend/src/components/SeedControlsWizard.jsx: New multi-step wizard — approach selection, department mapping, duplicate review (side-by-side with department filter), confirm & apply, done summary.
frontend/src/pages/DataTools.jsx: Added Standard Controls card with Seed Standard Controls button (Admin only); imports SeedControlsWizard.

user_bullets: Fixed: training videos now correctly show the full library for your role — previously all users saw only the Risk Champion tier regardless of actual role.
Fixed: Viewers no longer have access to the company-wide audit log.
Fixed: deleting a BCM process now correctly rejects attempts where the RTO exceeds the MTPD, even when the two values are in different time units.
bullets: SEC-06: Fixed req.company_role typo in 4 training route handlers — now correctly reads req.company.role. All roles now receive the correct tier of training videos.
SEC-07: Error handler gate changed from === 'staging' to !== 'production' — internal error details can no longer leak if a staging build is deployed to production.
SEC-08: _debug_cro gate hardened with the same !== 'production' pattern.
SEC-09: Removed user email (PII) from console.log in user-creation flow — logs now reference user ID only.

BCM-01: Deleted 7 orphaned BCM page files (BcmDashboard, BcmProcessRegister, BcmBcpLibrary, BcmTestingLog, BcmScenarioLibrary, BcmDependencyRegister, BcmActivationLog) — BCM is disconnected from the frontend; these were dead code in the Vite bundle.
RBAC-01: Company creation via /api/companies/standalone now requires is_consultant AND is_super_admin — is_consultant alone is no longer sufficient.
RBAC-02: Viewer role removed from /api/audit-log requireRole guard — audit log now requires Admin, Manager, Risk Champion, Approver, CRO, or Consultant CRO.
AUTH-04: LOCKOUT_MINUTES now passed as a query parameter (make_interval(mins => $2)) instead of string-interpolated into SQL.
OPS-01: SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINUTES corrected to 10 in deploy-staging.sh — now matches production default (was 30, 3× too long).
DEPLOY-01: Staging test API key no longer retrieved and printed to terminal — deploy output now shows the Secret Manager key name instead.
WS-01: BCM process RTO < MTPD validation now normalizes both values to minutes before comparing — cross-unit pairs (e.g. 72 hours vs 1 day) are now correctly caught.
files: server.js (SEC-06, SEC-07, SEC-08, SEC-09, RBAC-01, RBAC-02, WS-01)

user_bullets: Internal: BCM Activations now use soft-delete, consistent with all other BCM records — data is never permanently lost on deletion.
Internal: legacy duplicate API routes removed; docx dependency correctly available in production builds.
bullets: A-01: docx moved from devDependencies to dependencies — now correctly installed in Cloud Run production builds.

A-03: schema_v52 adds is_deleted to bcm_activations. DELETE endpoint converted from hard DELETE to soft UPDATE SET is_deleted=true. GET endpoints now filter WHERE is_deleted=false. BCM tables are now uniformly soft-deleted.

DC-04: Legacy /api/categories (GET/POST/DELETE) routes removed — fully superseded by /api/risk-taxonomy and /api/risk-sub-categories (Admin-managed, structured, with IDs). No frontend reference to /api/categories existed.

schema: v51 — role CHECK constraint updated to include all current roles
user_bullets: Fixed: uploading training videos with unusual filenames (including path traversal attempts) is now safely handled.
Fixed: deleting a risk category is now blocked if any of its sub-categories are in use by risks.
Fixed: the database role constraint now correctly reflects all user roles in the system.
bullets: SEC-05: Training upload-url endpoint now sanitizes filename using path.basename() — prevents path traversal via malformed filenames.
D-04: DELETE /api/risk-categories/:id now checks whether any risks reference sub-categories belonging to the category before allowing deletion (409 if in use).

S-04: schema_v51 drops the stale role CHECK constraint (only listed Admin/Manager/Viewer) and replaces it with the full current vocabulary: Admin, Manager, Viewer, Risk Champion, CRO, Consultant CRO, Approver.
T-02: test-bcm.js RBAC matrix extended with Consultant CRO — verifies 403 on all BCM GET and POST endpoints.
T-03: testAuditEdgeCases() in test-suite.js updated — link-risk test now explicitly documented as the T-03 cross-company isolation test.
E-04: refreshBcpTestSummary transactional pattern confirmed intentional — no code change needed.
files: server.js (SEC-05 filename sanitization, D-04 sub-category in-use check)

schema: v50 — company_id on mitigations, UNIQUE(company_id, mitigation_uid), Deferred CHECK constraint
user_bullets: Fixed: duplicate MAP IDs are now impossible — the database enforces that each MAP-NNNN is unique within your company.
Fixed: creating a Deferred MAP without specifying whether compensatory controls are in place is now blocked at every entry point, including when creating a risk with inline MAPs.
Fixed: the debug diagnostic field is no longer included in production API responses.
Fixed: deleting a glossary term or evidence file that doesn't exist now returns a proper 'not found' error instead of silently succeeding.
bullets: S-02/D-03: schema_v50 adds company_id column to mitigations (backfilled from risks), NOT NULL enforced, UNIQUE(company_id, mitigation_uid) constraint eliminates MAP UID race condition.
S-03: schema_v50 adds CHECK constraint — compensatory_controls_in_place IN ('Yes','No') when status='Deferred'.

D-02: Inline MAP creation in POST /api/risks now validates compensatory_controls_in_place for each Deferred MAP, matching the existing enforcement on the standalone MAP POST/PUT routes. company_id also now populated in inline INSERT.
Standalone MAP POST: company_id added to INSERT so the new NOT NULL constraint is satisfied.
SEC-04/DC-01: _debug_cro field now only included in /api/my-tasks response when NODE_ENV=staging — never exposed in production.
E-01: DELETE /api/glossary/:id returns 404 when term not found (was silently 200).
E-02: DELETE /api/evidence/:id returns 404 when file not found (was silently 200).

T-01: testAuditEdgeCases() added to test-suite.js — covers D-02 (Deferred MAP validation), SEC-02 (cross-company link-risk ownership), W-01 (CRO accept state guard), E-01 (glossary 404), E-02 (evidence 404).
files: server.js (D-02 inline MAP validation, SEC-04/DC-01 _debug_cro conditional, E-01/E-02 404 fixes, company_id in MAP INSERTs)

user_bullets: Security fix: eliminated SQL injection risk from department-scoping queries — company identifiers are now always passed as parameters, never interpolated into SQL strings.
Security fix: the 'link control to risk' action now verifies both the risk and the control belong to your company before linking — prevents cross-company data linkage.
Data integrity fix: creating a risk with a department name that doesn't exist in your company's department list is now rejected with a clear error message.
Documentation fix: confirmed users.id is an integer (SERIAL) — no code changes needed.
bullets: SEC-01: Replaced company_id = '${cid}' string interpolation with parameterized $1 in managerScopeClause and its two inline call sites (risks list query, my-tasks Manager queue). Removes SQL injection risk in all department code/name resolution subqueries.

SEC-02: POST /api/controls/:id/link-risk now performs a Promise.all ownership check — verifies risk_id belongs to req.company.id in risks table AND control_id belongs to req.company.id in controls_lib before INSERT. Returns 404 if either check fails.

D-01: POST /api/risks now validates req.body.department against the departments table (by code or name, case-insensitive) inside the transaction immediately after BEGIN. Returns 400 if the department doesn't exist for this company.
S-01: Confirmed users.id is SERIAL (INT) in schema_v2.sql — no GRC-side CLAUDE.md exists to correct; finding closed by inspection.
files: server.js (SEC-01: 6 interpolations → $1; SEC-02: ownership check; D-01: dept validation)

user_bullets: Security improvement: login, MFA, and password reset rate limits now work correctly when the app is running on multiple servers simultaneously — an attacker can no longer bypass the limit by spreading requests across replicas.
bullets: SEC-03: Replaced in-memory rate limiters for login/MFA/forgot-password/reset-password with a Postgres-backed makeDbRateLimiter. Single UPSERT with atomic window-reset resets the count at expiry without a separate cron. Fails open on DB error.
schema_v49: rate_limit_attempts table (key TEXT PK, count INT, window_start TIMESTAMPTZ) with index on window_start for cleanup.
apiLimiter (600 req/15 min global throttle) remains in-memory — adding 10–20 ms DB latency to every API call would be disproportionate for a general DDoS guard.
Background setInterval purges entries older than 1 hour every 10 minutes to keep the table small.

user_bullets: Fixed: Training videos are now properly access-controlled — a missing or invalid role can no longer receive a partial video list instead of being refused.
Fixed: Training videos for the Approver role now sort in the correct position in the training library.
Fixed: Requesting help for a page with no training video now returns a clear 'not found' response instead of an empty result.
bullets: R-02/R-04: Removed redundant authenticate middleware from ~40 routes already covered by the global app.use('/api', authenticate, ...) — no behaviour change, eliminates double-auth per request.
R-03: Training GET routes now include requireRole guard covering all valid roles — returns 403 instead of silently falling back to Risk Champion access level if role is unset.
DC-03: Added Approver to the ORDER BY CASE in GET /api/training/videos — was sorting as NULL (after Admin), now correctly sits between Risk Champion and Viewer.
E-03: GET /api/training/help/:pageId now returns 404 when no video is found for the page (was returning null with 200).

user_bullets: Fixed: CRO can no longer decline a risk that hasn't been Manager-approved — preventing a risk from accidentally skipping the approval chain.
Fixed: Re-opening and then re-approving a risk now correctly marks it as Active instead of leaving it in an undefined state.
Fixed: The CRO acceptance queue no longer shows risks that are still awaiting Manager approval.
Fixed: Issue closure and risk-acceptance sign-off now correctly enforces separation of duties against the person who raised the issue (not a deprecated field).
Fixed: Deferring a Mitigation Action Plan now requires a Yes/No answer on compensatory controls before saving.
bullets: W-01: cro-decline endpoint now rejects with 400 if approval_status != 'Approved' — prevents Draft/Awaiting risks from bypassing the Manager approval chain.
W-02: approve endpoint CASE WHEN risk_status = 'Re-opened' THEN 'Active' (was NULL) — fixes broken risk_status after re-approval.
W-03: GET /api/risks/pending-cro now filters AND approval_status = 'Approved' — removes pre-approval risks from CRO queue.
W-04: Issue SoD checks at /api/issues/:id/status now compare against created_by (not the deprecated owner field which is always null for manual issues).
W-05: POST /api/risks/:id/mitigations and PUT /api/mitigations/:id now return 400 if status=Deferred and compensatory_controls_in_place is not 'Yes' or 'No'.

schema: v48 — mitigations status constraint widened; compensatory_controls_in_place column added
user_bullets: Mitigation Action Plans (MAPs) now support two new statuses: Deferred and Cancelled. When Deferred, you will be asked whether compensatory controls are in place (Yes/No) — this is logged in the audit trail.
MAPs that are Pending or In Progress past their end date now show an Overdue badge in red, making it easy to spot at-risk actions.
You can now add, edit, or delete individual MAPs directly from the risk detail panel — no need to re-create the whole risk.
bullets: ENH-14: schema_v48 — mitigations status CHECK updated to include Deferred and Cancelled; compensatory_controls_in_place TEXT column added (CHECK 'Yes'/'No', NULL unless Deferred).
Backend: POST /api/risks/:id/mitigations, PUT /api/mitigations/:id, DELETE /api/mitigations/:id — all restricted to operational roles.
Audit log entries for map_create, map_update (with deferred details), and map_delete.
RiskDetail: MAP panel upgraded from read-only list to interactive cards with Edit/Delete buttons and + Add MAP.
Overdue badge: computed at render time (end_date < today AND status Pending/In Progress).
Status = Deferred triggers inline Yes/No compensatory controls question (shown in both NewRiskForm and MAP edit modal).
NewRiskForm: status dropdown updated with Deferred and Cancelled options + conditional compensatory controls radio.
files: server.js (MAP CRUD endpoints, attachControlsAndMitigations updated, risk creation updated)
frontend/src/pages/RiskRegister.jsx (RiskDetail MAP panel, NewRiskForm MAP section, modal)

user_bullets: Risk categories and sub-categories are now pre-loaded for every new company — 11 standard categories covering Strategic, Operational, Financial, Compliance, Technology, and more.
When creating a risk, you now select the sub-category from a dropdown filtered to the chosen category — no more free-text entry.

Admins can add, rename, or delete categories and sub-categories at any time from the new Risk Configuration page in the Admin panel. Deletion is blocked if any risks are currently using that category.
bullets: ENH-01/10: risk_sub_categories table added (schema_v47). FK to risk_categories with ON DELETE CASCADE.
SEED_TAXONOMY constant (11 categories, 68 sub-categories) added to server.js and migrate-all.js.
seedRiskTaxonomy() helper seeds all three company creation paths and backfills existing companies on migration.
GET /api/risk-taxonomy returns structured [{id, name, sub_categories}] for the current company.
POST/PUT/DELETE /api/risk-categories (Admin only) with in-use guard on delete.
POST/PUT/DELETE /api/risk-sub-categories (Admin only) with in-use guard on delete.
RiskConfig.jsx: new Admin page — expandable category list, inline rename, add/delete with error feedback.
RiskRegister.jsx NewRiskForm: category dropdown uses taxonomy objects; sub-category is now a filtered dropdown.
Layout.jsx: Risk Configuration added to Admin nav. App.jsx: route wired.
files: server.js (SEED_TAXONOMY, seedRiskTaxonomy, new endpoints, 3 company creation paths)

schema: v46 — user_companies.role CHECK constraint updated; all 'Submitter' rows renamed to 'Risk Champion'
user_bullets: The 'Submitter' role has been renamed to 'Risk Champion' throughout the app — in menus, labels, and access controls.
Existing users with the Submitter role have been automatically updated to Risk Champion — no action needed.
bullets: ENH-02: Full DB-level rename of 'Submitter' → 'Risk Champion' in user_companies.role.
schema_v46_rename_submitter.sql: drops old role CHECK constraint, renames existing rows, adds new constraint with 'Risk Champion'.
server.js: all role checks, requireRole() calls, validation messages, and comments updated.

Frontend: Layout.jsx, App.jsx, RiskRegister.jsx, UserManagement.jsx, MyTasks.jsx, OrgRoles.jsx, TrainingLibrary.jsx, TopBar.jsx, EvidenceAttachments.jsx, DepartmentField.jsx, help-content.js — all 'Submitter' references replaced.
test-suite.js, test-routing-audit.js, test-suite-extended.js, test-bcm.js updated.

user_bullets: A new 'What's New' button in the sidebar lets you see a plain-English summary of what has changed in each release.
The release log document is now generated automatically every time a new version is deployed — no manual updates needed.
bullets: changelog.json introduced as single source of truth with dual-tier content: user_bullets for the in-app What's New modal and full technical bullets + files for the Word doc.
WhatsNew.jsx modal component: reads changelog.json, shows last 3 versions' user_bullets, uses localStorage to track last-seen version.
Badge dot on 'What's New' sidebar button clears when user opens the modal.
scripts/generate-release-log.js: Node.js script (docx npm package) that reads changelog.json and regenerates GRC_App_Release_Log.docx automatically.
generate-release-log.js wired into deploy-staging.sh and deploy-certitude.sh as a pre-build step.
npm run release-log script added to package.json.

user_bullets: When you close an issue, the app now prompts you to review any linked risks — so risk records stay up to date.
If a closed issue has no linked risks, you'll get a reminder to check the Risk Register for any related risks worth updating.
bullets: ENH-15: PostClosurePrompt modal fires after Closed-Remediated or Risk Accepted status on an issue.
If issue has linked risks: lists each risk_uid and suggests reviewing ratings or lifecycle status.
If no linked risks: generic reminder to check the Risk Register.
Prompt is optional — user can dismiss or navigate directly to the Risk Register.
onNavigate prop threaded from App.jsx to IssuesTracker for Risk Register navigation.
StatusForm.onDone() now passes savedStatus so parent can detect closing statuses.
files: frontend/src/pages/IssuesTracker.jsx (PostClosurePrompt modal, onNavigate prop, StatusForm onDone)

user_bullets: Risks now show an Open, Closed, or Re-opened status alongside the existing approval workflow status.
Managers and CROs can close a risk (with a mandatory reason), and re-open it if circumstances change — the reason is recorded and shown on the risk.
Reopening a risk automatically sends it back through the approval workflow.
Each risk's detail view now shows any issues that have been linked to it.
bullets: ENH-08: New Status column on Risk Register showing Open / Closed / Re-opened badges; existing Status column renamed to Lifecycle Status.
Manager, CRO, Consultant CRO only can close or reopen risks; Admin and Submitter removed from close/reopen endpoints.
Closing a risk requires closure_reason (already existed); reopening requires reopen_reason (new column).
Reopen resets approval_status to Awaiting Approval and sets risk_status = 'Re-opened'.
Re-opened badge clears automatically to Open when risk is re-approved.
attachControlsAndMitigations: 5th parallel query added for linked_issues (reverse lookup via issue_risks).
Linked Issues read-only panel added to RiskDetail between Mitigations and Related Risks.
canManageLifecycle gate: role === 'Manager' || role === 'CRO' || role === 'Consultant CRO'.
files: server.js (close/reopen role gates, reopen_reason, Re-opened risk_status, approve clears Re-opened, attachControlsAndMitigations linked_issues, cloneRiskAsNewVersion reopen_reason)
frontend/src/pages/RiskRegister.jsx (StatusBadge, Lifecycle Status rename, linked issues panel, reopen reason form, canManageLifecycle)

schema: schema_v41_map_enhancements.sql (mitigations: mitigation_uid, action_owner, root_cause), schema_v42_issues_recurrence.sql (issues: is_recurring)
user_bullets: A new 'Directive' control type is now available when adding controls to risks.

'Not yet tested' is now a valid effectiveness result for controls — useful for newly implemented controls that haven't been through a test cycle yet. Controls with this result will not trigger a remediation issue.
Mitigation actions now have a unique reference number (e.g. MAP-0001), an action owner, and a root cause field for clearer accountability and tracking.
You can now flag an issue as recurring to track patterns and repeat problems. A filter lets you view only recurring issues.
bullets: ENH-04: 'Directive' added as control type in inline quick-add panel and risk create form Step controls.
ENH-05: 'Not yet tested' added as valid control effectiveness result; excluded from remediation and needs_reassessment triggers.
ENH-11: Mitigation Action Plan unique ID (MAP-NNNN) — company-scoped sequential via generateMitigationUID() using REGEXP_REPLACE.
ENH-12: action_owner TEXT column added to mitigations.
ENH-13: root_cause TEXT column added to mitigations.
ENH-16: is_recurring BOOLEAN added to issues; checkbox on IssueForm, badge on issue row, recurring-only filter button.
files: server.js (ENH-05 result validation, ENH-11 generateMitigationUID, ENH-16 is_recurring INSERT/PATCH)
frontend/src/pages/RiskRegister.jsx (ENH-04 Directive option, ENH-11/12/13 MAP card rewrite with action owner + root cause)

user_bullets: Fixed an issue where the owner department was not pre-filling correctly when logging a new issue.
Minor display improvements to department and business unit selectors across the app.
bullets: P0: Removed /api/debug endpoint (security exposure — returned raw environment variables).
P1: Submitter dept picker bug fixed — owner department now pre-selected correctly on issue create form.
P2: Version display in UI header corrected to reflect current package.json version.
P3: RiskRegister subtitle text corrected.
Fixed 3 cosmetic issues in DepartmentField and CascadingDeptSelector: label alignment, empty state placeholder, sub-dept display in Simple Mode.

user_bullets: Fixed a critical issue where business unit permissions were lost when a page was refreshed, causing some users to lose access to their assigned records.
Fixed an error that occurred when loading users who were assigned to business units set up before the current version.
bullets: CRITICAL fix: GET /api/users SELECT was missing business_unit_ids column — BU scope lost on page reload, returning 500 errors for BU-scoped users.
HIGH fix: ::int[] cast on legacy BU code data throwing 500 — replaced with safe COALESCE + integer coercion.
files: server.js (GET /api/users SELECT, BU int cast safety)

user_bullets: Fixed the compliance calendar — managers now only see calendar items relevant to their department scope.
Several minor stability improvements to approval and status change workflows.
bullets: Bug fix: GET /api/calendar compliance calendar dept scoping updated for BU-scoped Managers.
Fixed 6 low/minor issues from workflow audit: approval status guards, edge cases in status transitions, UI label inconsistencies.
files: server.js (GET /api/calendar dept scoping, misc workflow guards)

user_bullets: Fixed business unit permissions for managers — access is now correctly applied based on your assigned business units.
Fixed an error that prevented the department selector from loading correctly when assigning managers.
bullets: Bug fix: UserManagement.jsx storing BU codes instead of BU integer IDs in user_companies.business_unit_ids.
Bug fix: GET /api/departments/without-manager updated to correctly resolve BU-scoped Managers.
Bug fix: BcmProcessRegister.jsx department code vs. integer FK mismatch resolved.

user_bullets: In Business Unit Mode, the Business Unit and Department are now shown as separate, clearly labeled fields when creating risks, issues, KRIs, and controls — making it easier to assign records to the right part of your organisation.
bullets: Business Unit field split in entity create forms: Risk, Issues, KRI, and Control registers now show Business Unit and Department as separate labeled fields in BU Mode.
Improved UX in BU Mode by making BU selection explicit on all entity create/edit forms.
files: frontend/src/pages/RiskRegister.jsx (BU field split on create form)

user_bullets: Unique reference IDs are now applied consistently across all record types — issues, compliance obligations, policies, and business continuity plans all follow the same structured format.
The Business Unit mode setting can now be changed after a company is first created, directly from the Company Profile page.
bullets: Gap fix: universal 4-part ID format extended to Issues (ISS-), Obligations (OBL-), Policies (POL-), and all BCM entities.
Added generateUnique4PartID() helper — replaces generateUniqueUid() for all entity types.
Gap fix: has_business_units now editable on existing companies via Company Profile page.
GET /api/companies/current/profile now returns has_business_units.
PUT /api/companies/current/profile now accepts has_business_units (COALESCE — omit to leave unchanged).
Companies.jsx: BU Mode toggle checkbox added to profile edit form with live warning on mode switch.
files: server.js (generateUnique4PartID, all entity UID call sites, GET/PUT /api/companies/current/profile)
frontend/src/pages/Companies.jsx (BU Mode toggle on profile form)

schema: schema_v45_business_units.sql (business_units table, has_business_units on companies, BU columns on departments and user_companies)
user_bullets: Business Unit Mode is now available — companies can be structured with multiple business units, each with their own departments and management scope.
All records (risks, controls, issues, etc.) now use a structured reference ID that reflects your company's department structure (e.g. RI-FIN-AR-0001).
Managers can be scoped to specific business units, so each manager only sees the risks and issues relevant to their area.
bullets: Major release: Multi-Business-Unit architecture.
Company mode toggle: BU Mode (Yes) or Simple Mode (No).
New business_units table; has_business_units flag on companies.
business_unit_id + parent_dept_id columns on departments; business_unit_ids on user_companies.
Extended getManagerDepts() to resolve BU → Dept hierarchy.
Extended Approver lookup to cascade upward: sub-dept → parent dept → skip.
Universal 4-part ID format for all entities (RI-SLOT1-SLOT2-NNNN).
Cascading BU→Dept filter (BU Mode) and Dept→Sub-dept filter (Simple Mode) on all registers.
Admin: Business Units CRUD page, dept BU linkage, sub-dept parent linkage, BU scope on User Management.
files: server.js (resolveIDSlots, generateUnique4PartID, BU API routes, getUserCompanies)

user_bullets: When sending a risk back for revision, you now enter your reason in a proper form panel — no more browser popup dialogs.
bullets: Replaced window.prompt send-back dialogs in MyTasks.jsx with proper modal UI.
handleManagerReject and handleApproverReject now open an in-page modal with reason textarea, busy state, and error display.

user_bullets: Fixed several issues with the Manager role: approvals, send-backs, and risks imported by an admin now flow through the correct workflow steps.
bullets: Fixed 5 Manager role bugs: import handlers using legacy dept field; Accept/Avoid risks bypassing CRO queue on import; approve endpoint missing status guard; Admin Approve button causing 403s; window.prompt send-back modals in RiskRegister.jsx.

user_bullets: CROs can now send back risks they have been asked to accept or avoid, with a reason recorded for transparency.
bullets: CRO Acceptance workflow: CRO can decline (send back) Accept/Avoid risks.
New /api/risks/:id/cro-decline endpoint.
Send Back button added to CRO Acceptance queue in MyTasks.jsx.
cro_acceptance_status supports pending_cro, accepted, declined.

user_bullets: A new Approver role is available as an optional step between the person who submits a risk and the manager who reviews it.
If no Approver is assigned to a department, the workflow automatically skips to the Manager — no configuration needed.
bullets: Approver role introduced as optional workflow layer between Submitter and Manager.
approver_email and approved_at_approver columns added to risks.
Approver can approve or send back risks; if no Approver assigned, workflow skips to Manager.
Training Videos: role_level extended to include Approver.

schema: schema_v19_additions.sql (impact pillar columns on risks, pillar definitions in company_settings)
user_bullets: Risk impact can now be scored across up to five dimensions: Financial, Operational, Reputational, Regulatory, and Strategic.
The highest-scoring dimension (the 'governing pillar') is shown as a badge on each risk so reviewers know which area is most affected.
bullets: Multi-pillar impact scoring system across up to 5 pillars.
Governing pillar badge displayed on Risk Register.
Pillar definitions stored in company_settings, configurable per company.
ScoringMethodology admin page updated with pillar accordion and updated likelihood definitions.

schema: schema_v34–v44 (risk_library, benchmarking, maturity_assessment, company_profile, training_videos, evidence_board_approval)
user_bullets: Board approval decisions can now include file attachments as supporting evidence.
Training videos are now available directly in the app, filtered to show only content relevant to your role.
New Maturity Assessment module — benchmark your GRC programme against industry standards.
A GCC industry risk and control library is available as a reference when building out your own register.
bullets: Evidence Attachments extended with board_approval entity type.
Training Videos module added with role-level gating.
Company Profile page.
Maturity Assessment module.
Consultant Benchmarking module.
GCC Industry Risk & Control Library (155 risks, 105 controls) seeded as global reference data.

schema: schema_v17–v33 (group structure, MFA, email reset, BCM tables, risk-BCP links)
user_bullets: Business Continuity Management is now available — create and manage BCP plans, recovery scenarios, test records, and activations.
Risks can now be linked to BCP plans, so you can see which continuity plans are in place for each risk.
Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is now supported for added account security.
Password reset via email is now available if you forget your credentials.
bullets: BCM module: BCP Plans, BCP Tests, Scenarios, Dependencies, Activations.
Risk ↔ BCP linkage.
Group/Subsidiary company structure (parent_company_id).
Industry field on companies.
MFA (Multi-Factor Authentication).
Email-based password reset.

schema: schema_v7–v16 (department columns, escalation_rules, risk enhancements, glossary, evidence_attachments, role_governance)
user_bullets: Controls, KRIs, and issues are now scoped to departments — each team sees only what's relevant to them.
A new Risk Appetite score and Risk Velocity field are now available on each risk.
Supporting documents and evidence can now be attached directly to risks, controls, issues, obligations, and KRIs.
A Glossary page has been added for consistent terminology across the platform.
Escalation rules can now be configured to automatically escalate risks above a set threshold.
bullets: Department scoping for Controls, KRIs, and Issues.
Escalation Rules engine (threshold-based auto-escalation).
Risk appetite score, risk velocity, risk taxonomy terms (cause/consequence).
Corrective control type added.
KRI threshold bands (JSONB) for traffic-light display.
Glossary and company settings pages.
Evidence attachments on risks, controls, issues, obligations, KRIs.
Consultant CRO role introduced.

schema: schema_v2–v6 (multi-tenant, core tables, controls_lib, compliance_obligations, issues, department columns)
user_bullets: Initial launch: Risk Register, Control Library, Compliance Obligations, Issues & Actions Tracker, and KRI modules.
Department-scoped roles — Submitter, Manager, CRO, Viewer, and Admin — so each person sees only what they need to.
A full audit log records all significant changes across the platform.
bullets: Initial multi-tenant platform launch.
Core modules: Risk Register, Controls Library, Compliance Obligations, Issues, KRIs.
RACI matrix on risks and controls.
Org Roles management (RACI).
Policies and policy linkages.
Department-scoped user roles: Submitter, Manager, CRO, Viewer, Admin.
Email-based authentication with session management.
Audit log on all material changes.
Per-company user management.
